---
title: "3.9 this Keyword"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## `this` Keyword

### `this` refers to the current object

Within an instance method or constructor, <span class="term">`this`</span> is a reference to the object whose method or constructor is running.

::: {.tight-list}
- if `p1.setEmail()` is called, `this` refers to `p1`
- if `p2.setEmail()` is called, `this` refers to `p2`
- the current object changes with the receiver of the call
:::

## Where `this` Can Be Used

### It exists only in instance context

The textbook is explicit here:

::: {.tight-list}
- `this` can be used in <span class="term">instance methods</span>
- `this` can be used in <span class="term">constructors</span>
- class methods do <span class="mark">not</span> have a `this` reference
:::

Static methods are called with the class name, so there is no current object.

## `this.instanceVariable`

### Use `this` to distinguish fields from parameters

```java
public Person(String name)
{
    this.name = name;
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- the parameter `name` is the closest local variable
- `this.name` means the instance variable in the current object
- this resolves the naming conflict clearly
:::

This is the natural follow-up to the scope-shadowing rule from Topic 3.8.

## `this` as an Argument

### The current object can be passed to another method or constructor

![Tracing this as a shared reference](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-9-this-keyword/thisTrace.png){fig-align="center" width="76%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- `this` can be used anywhere an object variable could be used
- passing `this` passes the current object reference
- multiple names may then refer to the same object
:::

This topic ties back to the object-reference ideas from 3.6.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

![Bank account challenge prompt](../../assets/3-class-creation/3-9-this-keyword/dollarSign.png){fig-align="center" width="34%"}

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- identify what object `this` refers to in a constructor or instance method
- distinguish `name` from `this.name` when parameters shadow fields
- explain why static methods cannot use `this`
- trace `this` when passed as an argument
- <span class="term">3.9.3 Coding Challenge: Bank Account</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define `this` as a reference to the current object
- state that `this` is available only in constructors and instance methods
- explain how `this.instanceVariable` resolves a naming conflict
- explain what happens when `this` is passed as an argument
- connect `this` back to object-reference tracing
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
